title: Mass spectrometry system and method with stacked-ring ion transfer device
abstract: A mass spectrometry system that includes an ion source that produces ions, an ion transfer device, and a mass analyzer. The ion transfer device includes a first ion transfer device that receives ions from an inlet and transfers ions to an outlet, and includes a plurality of first plate electrodes that are stacked, each plate electrode having a hole with a same diameter, and separated by a first inter-electrode spacing such that the hole diameter is between 1 to 100 times the first inter-electrode spacing; a second ion transfer device receives and transfers the ions, and includes a plurality of second plate electrodes with holes having a same diameter, separated by a second inter-electrode spacing such that the hole diameter is between 3 to 100 times the second inter-electrode spacing; and a third ion transfer device includes a plurality of third plate electrodes with holes having a plurality of diameters.
claims: 1. A mass spectrometry system comprising: an ion source that produces ions; an ion transfer device including: a first ion transfer device that receives the ions from an inlet of the first ion transfer device and transfers the ions to an outlet of the first ion transfer device, wherein the first ion transfer device includes a plurality of first plate electrodes that are stacked, each plate electrode of the plurality of first plate electrodes having a hole, the holes of the plurality of first plate electrodes having a same diameter, each plate electrode of the plurality of first plate electrodes being separated from each adjacent plate electrode of the plurality of first plate electrodes by a first inter-electrode spacing, and wherein the diameter of the hole of each of the plurality of first plate electrodes is between 1 to 100 times the first inter-electrode spacing of the plurality of first plate electrodes, a second ion transfer device that receives the ions from an inlet of the second ion transfer device and transfers the ions to an outlet of the second ion transfer device, wherein the second ion transfer device includes a plurality of second plate electrodes that are stacked, each plate electrode of the plurality of second plate electrodes having a hole, the holes of the plurality of second plate electrodes having a same diameter, each plate electrode of the plurality of second plate electrodes being separated from each adjacent plate electrode of the plurality of second plate electrodes by a second inter-electrode spacing, and wherein the diameter of the hole of each of the plurality of second plate electrodes is between 3 to 100 times the second inter-electrode spacing of the plurality of second plate electrodes, and a third ion transfer device that receives the ions from an inlet of the third ion transfer device and transfers the ions to an outlet of the third ion transfer device, wherein the third ion transfer device includes a plurality of third plate electrodes that are stacked, each plate electrode of the plurality of third plate electrodes having a hole, the holes of the plurality of third plate electrodes having a plurality of diameters, each plate electrode of the plurality of third plate electrodes being separated from each adjacent plate electrode of the plurality of third plate electrodes by a third inter-electrode spacing, wherein each of the plurality of diameters of each of the holes of the plurality of third plate electrodes is between 3 to 100 times the third inter-electrode spacing of the plurality of third plate electrodes; and a mass analyzer that separates the ions based on mass-to-charge ratio of the ions.
